text: HelixDB is an open-source graph-vector database built on Rust. It provides a unified platform combining graph database capabilities with vector search functionality. The storage engine uses LMDB for efficient memory-mapped operations.

HelixQL is the query language for HelixDB. It features type-safe declarations, compile-time checking, and support for nodes, edges, and vector embeddings. The syntax is declarative and inspired by GraphQL and Cypher.
Vector search in databases enables semantic similarity queries. Unlike traditional keyword search, vector search finds items based on meaning by comparing embedding vectors in high-dimensional space.
Graph databases excel at representing connected data. Nodes represent entities, edges represent relationships, and traversals efficiently navigate these connections without expensive JOIN operations.
LMDB (Lightning Memory-Mapped Database) is the storage backend for HelixDB. It provides ACID transactions, memory-mapped I/O for speed, and multi-reader single-writer access patterns.
Fine-tuning language models on domain-specific data improves their performance for specialized tasks. QLoRA enables efficient fine-tuning by using 4-bit quantization and Low-Rank Adaptation.
MLOps encompasses the practices for deploying and maintaining machine learning models in production. Key aspects include data versioning, experiment tracking, CI/CD pipelines, and model monitoring.
Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) teaches models to follow instructions by training on input-output pairs. Chain-of-Thought (CoT) training adds reasoning traces to improve complex problem solving.

The Transformer architecture revolutionized NLP with its self-attention mechanism. Models like GPT, BERT, and LLaMA build on this foundation with different pretraining objectives and architectural choices.
Embedding models convert text to dense vector representations. These vectors capture semantic meaning, enabling similarity search, clustering, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) applications.
